english: the shape of a basket repeatedly woven from bamboo

etymology.description: йӯҡ(йӯҡ) (yГә, "fish") + йӯҡ(йӯҡ) (yГә, "fish") facing each other = original script of йҒҳ(йҒҳ) (gГІu, "to meet")
etymology.interpretation: The character еҶ“ represents two fish facing each other, symbolizing the act of meeting or encountering, which reflects its connection to the meaning of йҒҳ(йҒҳ) (gГІu, "to meet"). This imagery of fish is traditionally associated with gatherings, thus extending the sense of "meeting" to the character. The later interpretations that depict accumulation are results of character form corruption after the Western Zhou period, rather than the original conception.
